title: Ports, Immutable Domain Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds explicitly owned TypeScript service graph from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of explicitly owned TypeScript service graph while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Direct domain, application, fetch, parser, repository, scheduler, HTTP, and presentation dependencies through narrow typed ports.
Represent admitted identifiers, instants, URLs, states, and results as readonly values created only after runtime checks.
Assign every Promise a caller, awaited settlement, cancellation policy, error path, concurrency budget, and shutdown consequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Directed Dependencies
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 1500 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove directed ports, immutable admission, Promise ownership, AbortSignal trees, and handle closure; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Directed Dependencies.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for direct domain, application, fetch, parser, repository, scheduler, http, and presentation dependencies through narrow typed ports. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Directed Dependencies, keep Feed Ts Directed Dependencies connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
